Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. AyaFlow: A high-performance, eBPF-based network traffic analyzer written in Rust 57 upvotes, 4 comments
  2. Pretext: TypeScript library for multiline text measurement and layout 116 upvotes, 18 comments
  3. Show HN: Create a full language server in Go with 3.17 spec support 73 upvotes, 14 comments
  4. Show HN: QuickBEAM – run JavaScript as supervised Erlang/OTP processes 44 upvotes, 7 comments
  5. Full network of clitoral nerves mapped out for first time 140 upvotes, 37 comments
  6. The RISE RISC-V Runners: free, native RISC-V CI on GitHub 80 upvotes, 20 comments
  7. Kyushu Railway Company Train Varieties 9 upvotes, 0 comments
  8. I turned my Kindle into my own personal newspaper 161 upvotes, 52 comments
  9. The rise and fall of IBM's 4 Pi aerospace computers: an illustrated history 46 upvotes, 11 comments
  10. Police used AI facial recognition to wrongly arrest TN woman for crimes in ND 250 upvotes, 97 comments
  11. Netscape News Feed Straight Out of the Late 00s 45 upvotes, 12 comments
  12. Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics 457 upvotes, 193 comments
  13. More on Version Control 19 upvotes, 3 comments
  14. The Cognitive Dark Forest 32 upvotes, 8 comments
  15. Voyager 1 runs on 69 KB of memory and an 8-track tape recorder 255 upvotes, 109 comments
  16. Neovim 0.12.0 164 upvotes, 74 comments
  17. Creating West Coast Buddhism (2024) 16 upvotes, 3 comments
  18. A nearly perfect USB cable tester 246 upvotes, 127 comments
  19. Observations from carbon dioxide monitoring 17 upvotes, 4 comments
  20. LinkedIn uses 2.4 GB RAM across two tabs 464 upvotes, 287 comments
  21. C++26 is done ISO C++ standards meeting, Trip Report 97 upvotes, 52 comments
  22. The "Vibe Coding" Wall of Shame 44 upvotes, 23 comments
  23. Show HN: BreezePDF – Free, in-browser PDF editor 43 upvotes, 24 comments
  24. Miasma: A tool to trap AI web scrapers in an endless poison pit 240 upvotes, 187 comments
  25. My MacBook Keyboard Is Broken and It's Insanely Expensive to Fix 41 upvotes, 28 comments
  26. The Epistemology of Microphysics 22 upvotes, 14 comments
  27. Midnight train from GA: A view of America from the tracks as airports struggle 9 upvotes, 4 comments
  28. Show HN: Sheet Ninja – Google Sheets as a CRUD Back End for Vibe Coders 57 upvotes, 66 comments
  29. Show HN: I made a "programming language" looking for feedback 6 upvotes, 3 comments
  30. Typing and Keyboards 29 upvotes, 36 comments

Last generated 4:30am, 30 March 2026. Source here.
